content.problemStatement:
Chef is a professional tennis player, and his aim is to win more Grand Slams than anyone ever has!

Currently, Chef has won XXX Grand Slams in total.

Chef needs to win **at least** 252525 Grand Slams in total to break the record for most total wins.

Every year, there are exactly 444 Grand Slams. Assuming Chef is skilled enough to always win every Grand Slam, what's the minimum number of years Chef needs to break the record?


Input Format

The first and only line of input will contain a single integer XXX, Chef's current number of Slams.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of years Chef needs to break the record for most Grand Slam wins.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Chef currently has X=0X = 0X=0 wins.
Every year, he can get 444 wins. So, after 777 years, he can have upto 4×7=284\times 7 = 284×7=28 wins which is more than the 252525 he needs.
666 years is not enough since only 242424 wins are attainable, so the answer is 777.

Explanation:
Chef currently has X=9X = 9X=9 wins.
Four more years, each with 444 wins, will give him another 4×4=164\times 4 = 164×4=16 wins. That makes a total of 9+16=259 + 16 = 259+16=25 wins, which is enough.
